items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Identity` **Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` **Triggered by:** PR #57200 by `@m-nash` ## What Changed PR #57200 moved all 59 public Azure.Identity types into `Azure.Core` and converted `Azure.Identity` into a lightweight `TypeForwardedTo` facade. Starting with version 1.53, credential types such as `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, `ClientSecretCredential`, etc. live in `Azure.Core` and are available to any project referencing `Azure.Core 1.53+` without a direct `Azure.Identity` dependency. A `MigrationGuide.md` was created at `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md` and CHANGELOG entries were added to both packages, but the main README was not updated. ## Gaps Found - The `README.md` "Install the package" section still instructs all users to `dotnet add package Azure.Identity` with no note that `Azure.Core 1.53+` already includes all credential types. - No mention of the type consolidation or the architectural change anywhere in the README. - No link to `MigrationGuide.md` from the README. - The "Getting started" / "Prerequisites" section contains no note that projects already on `Azure.Core 1.53+` can use credentials without adding an explicit `Azure.Identity` PackageReference. - The `Azure.Core` README (`sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md`) does not mention that it now bundles the full credential surface (`DefaultAzureCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, etc.) starting with 1.53.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` — edit In the **"Install the package"** section (under `## Getting started`), replace the existing install block with an expanded version that includes a note about `Azure.Core 1.53+`: ```markdown ### Install the package Install the Azure Identity client library for .NET with NuGet: ```dotnetcli dotnet add package Azure.Identity ``` > **Note:** Starting with `Azure.Core` 1.53.0, all credential types (including `DefaultAzureCredential`) are bundled directly in `Azure.Core`. If your project already targets `Azure.Core` 1.53.0 or later and you do not use any `Azure.Identity`-specific APIs, you can omit the `Azure.Identity` package reference entirely. See the [Migration Guide](./MigrationGuide.md) for upgrade and compatibility details. ``` In the **"Key concepts"** section, add a link to the migration guide at the end of the `### Credentials` subsection: ```markdown For details on the assembly consolidation introduced in version 1.53 and how to handle `CS0433` conflicts when mixing package versions, see the [Migration Guide](./MigrationGuide.md). ``` #### File 2: `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` — edit In the **"Key concepts"** bullet list (after the `TokenCredentials` bullet), add a new bullet: ```markdown - Authenticating Azure SDK clients using credential types from Microsoft Entra ID without a separate package dependency. (`DefaultAzureCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, and all other credential types from `Azure.Identity` are included directly in `Azure.Core` starting with version 1.53.) ``` Optionally add an **"Authentication"** sub-section in the Examples area pointing to the bundled credential types: ```markdown ### Authenticating Service Clients Starting with Azure.Core 1.53, all credential types previously in `Azure.Identity` are available directly: ```C# Snippet:AzureCoreDefaultCredential // No Azure.Identity package reference required for Azure.Core 1.53+ var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var client = new SecretClient(new Uri("(myvault.vault.azure.net/redacted), credential); ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet `AzureCoreDefaultCredential` referenced in Step 1 must be backed by a test file: **Test file path:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/tests/Samples/Sample_DefaultCredential.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using Azure.Identity; using Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets; using NUnit.Framework; using System; namespace Azure.Core.Tests.Samples { public class Sample_DefaultCredential { [Test] [Ignore("Sample only")] public void AuthenticateWithDefaultCredential() { #region Snippet:AzureCoreDefaultCredential // No Azure.Identity package reference required for Azure.Core 1.53+ var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var client = new SecretClient(new Uri("(myvault.vault.azure.net/redacted), credential); #endregion } } } ``` **README placeholder location:** in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md`, inside the new "Authentication" sub-section under `## Examples`, insert: ```` ```C# Snippet:AzureCoreDefaultCredential ``` ```` The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will inject the actual code between the fences. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Both READMEs already contain all required sections in the correct order. Only additions are needed; no sections are missing or out of order. `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` — check that the following appear and are complete: 1. Getting started ✅ (add install note per Step 1) 2. Key concepts ✅ (add migration guide link per Step 1) 3. Examples ✅ 4. Managed identity support ✅ 5. Troubleshooting ✅ 6. Next steps ✅ 7. Contributing ✅ `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` — check that the following appear and are complete: 1. Getting started ✅ 2. Key concepts ✅ (add bundled credentials bullet per Step 1) 3. Examples ✅ (add authentication section per Step 1) 4. Troubleshooting ✅ 5. Next steps ✅ 6. Contributing ✅ ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next. 1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/` 4. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 5. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` — check `git diff` to confirm README was updated 6. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` — check `git diff` to confirm README was updated 7. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 identity` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff 8. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core` — no public API changes expected; verify no diff 9. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` 10. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Identity] Docs: README does not reflect Azure.Core 1.53 type consolidation
